title: CAST and CONVERT
content:
### CAST and CONVERT Functions

CAST and CONVERT convert a value from one data type to another. CAST is SQL standard, while CONVERT provides additional formatting options.

---

### CAST Syntax

```sql
-- Convert string to integer
SELECT CAST('123' AS INTEGER);

-- Convert string to decimal
SELECT CAST('99.99' AS DECIMAL(10,2));

-- Convert number to string
SELECT CAST(42 AS VARCHAR(10));

-- Convert string to date
SELECT CAST('2024-01-15' AS DATE);
```

### CONVERT Syntax

CONVERT is available in SQL Server and MySQL with slightly different syntax:

```sql
-- SQL Server: CONVERT(target_type, expression [, style])
SELECT CONVERT(VARCHAR(10), GETDATE(), 101) AS us_date;
SELECT CONVERT(DECIMAL(10,2), '123.456');

-- MySQL: CONVERT(expression, target_type)
SELECT CONVERT('123', SIGNED INTEGER);
```

### Common Conversion Scenarios

```sql
-- Concatenating different types
SELECT 'Employee: ' || CAST(id AS VARCHAR) || ': ' || name
FROM employees;

-- Truncating decimal values
SELECT CAST(price AS INTEGER) AS price_whole
FROM products;

-- Safe comparison across types
SELECT * FROM orders
WHERE CAST(order_total AS DECIMAL(10,2)) > 100.00;
```

### Conversion Compatibility

| From | To | Notes |
|------|-----|-------|
| VARCHAR | INTEGER | Must contain valid number, error otherwise |
| VARCHAR | DATE | Must match expected date format |
| FLOAT | INTEGER | Truncates decimal portion |
| DATE | VARCHAR | Depends on database format settings |

> **Key Point:** CAST is portable across databases. CONVERT is database-specific but offers additional style formatting parameters.
codeExample:
SELECT name,
       CAST(salary AS DECIMAL(10,2)) AS precise_salary,
       CAST(hire_date AS VARCHAR(10)) AS hire_text
FROM employees;

quiz.question: Which conversion function is part of the SQL standard?
What happens when CAST('abc' AS INTEGER) is executed?
